records.ety: [L. destitutus, p. p. of destituere to set away, leave alone, forsake; de + statuere to set. See Statute.]

defs: Forsaken; not having in possession (something necessary, or desirable); deficient; lacking; devoid; -- often followed by of.
quotes: In thee is my trust; leave not my soul destitute.
Totally destitute of all shadow of influence.

defs: Not possessing the necessaries of life; in a condition of want; needy; without possessions or resources; very poor.
quotes: They wandered about in sheepskins and goatskins; being destitute, afflicted, tormented.

defs: To leave destitute; to forsake; to abandon.
quotes: To forsake or destitute a plantation.

defs: To make destitute; to cause to be in want; to deprive; -- followed by of.
quotes: Destituted of all honor and livings.
